7 Best Free AI Code Assistants for VS Code in 2026

Don't want to pay for AI coding? Here are the best free AI code assistants that won't cost you a dime.

A

AI Tools Team

Published July 6, 2026

Featured Tools (7)

#1

Codeium

Best Overall Free

Free AI code completion with unlimited suggestions across 70+ languages.

#2

Cody by Sourcegraph

Best Context

AI coding assistant with excellent codebase search and context awareness.

#3

Amazon CodeWhisperer

Best for Security

Amazon's free AI coding tool with strong security scanning.

#4

Tabnine

Best for Privacy

Privacy-focused AI code completion with a generous free tier.

#5

Continue.dev

Best Open Source

Open-source AI code assistant that works with any model.

#6

Aider

Best Terminal Tool

Terminal-based AI coding tool that edits your code via chat.

#7

ChatGPT

Most Versatile

Use ChatGPT's free tier for coding questions and code generation.

Not everyone wants to pay $10-20/month for AI coding assistance. The good news? There are several excellent free options that provide real value. Here are the best free AI code assistants available in 2026.

How We Evaluated

We tested each tool on:

  • Code completion quality — How accurate and useful are the suggestions?
  • Language support — How many programming languages are supported?
  • Free tier limits — What do you get without paying?
  • Ease of setup — How quickly can you start using it?

The Best Free AI Code Assistants

1. Codeium — Best Overall Free

Codeium offers unlimited AI code completions for free across 70+ languages. It works in VS Code, JetBrains, and other editors. The free tier is remarkably generous — you get the same core AI as the paid plan, just with fewer advanced features.

Free tier: Unlimited completions, basic chat, all languages.

2. Cody by Sourcegraph — Best Context

Cody uses Sourcegraph’s powerful codebase search to provide context-aware suggestions. It understands your entire repository, not just the current file. The free tier includes unlimited chat and completions.

Free tier: Unlimited completions and chat, basic context.

3. Amazon CodeWhisperer — Best for Security

Amazon’s free AI coding tool includes built-in security scanning that detects vulnerabilities as you code. It’s particularly strong for AWS-related development.

Free tier: Unlimited completions, security scanning, AWS integration.

4. Tabnine — Best for Privacy

Tabnine offers on-device AI models that run locally, keeping your code private. The free tier includes basic completions powered by their cloud model.

Free tier: Basic completions, limited to popular languages.

5. Continue.dev — Best Open Source

Continue is fully open-source and lets you connect any AI model — local or cloud. Use it with Ollama, OpenAI, Anthropic, or any compatible API. Maximum flexibility.

Free tier: Completely free (open source), bring your own API key.

6. Aider — Best Terminal Tool

Aider is a command-line AI coding tool that edits your code through conversation. It works with any terminal and supports multiple AI providers. Great for developers who prefer the command line.

Free tier: Free tool (bring your own API key).

7. ChatGPT — Most Versatile

ChatGPT’s free tier can answer coding questions, generate code, and explain concepts. It’s not an IDE integration, but it’s incredibly versatile for learning and debugging.

Free tier: GPT-3.5 access, limited GPT-4 usage.

👉 Try Cursor → 👉 Try GitHub Copilot → 👉 Try Codeium → 👉 Try Tabnine →

AFFILIATE_LINK

Related Articles

  • Best GitHub Copilot Alternatives — More options including paid tools
  • Best AI Coding Assistants — Our top picks for all budgets

FAQ

Are free AI code assistants as good as paid ones?

Free tools like Codeium and Cody are surprisingly capable. For most developers, they provide 80% of the value at 0% of the cost. Paid tools like Cursor and Copilot offer more advanced features, but free options are excellent for getting started.

Can I use multiple free assistants?

Yes! You can install Codeium and Cody simultaneously, though using one at a time is usually less confusing.

Do free tools keep my code private?

Privacy varies by tool. Tabnine and Continue.dev offer local processing options. Cloud-based tools like Codeium and Cody have privacy policies you should review.

Which free tool is best for beginners?

Codeium is the easiest to set up and use. Install the VS Code extension and start getting suggestions immediately.
